title: Jordan: The Ministry of Energy and Mineral Resources Signs Memorandum of Understanding With China's UEG to Study the Feasibility of Green Hydrogen
content:
Jordanâ€™s Ministry of Energy and Mineral Resources signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with UEG Green Development Holding Limited of China to conduct a feasibility study for a green hydrogen production project valued at US $1.155 billion. The MoU was signed by Minister Dr. Saleh Al-Kharabsheh and UEG Vice President Mr. Lei Guimin. The project is designed to produce about 200,000 tons of green ammonia annually for export using desalinated seawater and renewable energy from prime locations in Jordan at minimum cost.

Dr. Al-Kharabsheh emphasized the importance of the agreement in advancing Jordanâ€™s clean energy transition and its potential to improve the investment climate for green hydrogen projects. He confirmed the Ministryâ€™s readiness to cooperate with UEG on related energy fields, including gas and petroleum, and to facilitate the technical and economic studies required. Mr. Lei Guimin thanked the Ministry for its trust and highlighted UEGâ€™s regional experience, including operations in Iraq, which positions the company to work effectively in Jordan. The study will also determine the land requirements and technical phases of the project, supporting greater private sector involvement and laying the groundwork for a large-scale green hydrogen industry in Jordan.
author: Analysts Account
source: Ministry of Energy and Mineral Resources, Jordan
